entities.title: Architecture Is Communication
entities.summary: Architecture matters when it helps the next person understand why a system is shaped the way it is.
body: Architecture is not only the arrangement of components. It is the record of what a system believes about change.

A boundary explains which decisions belong together. A lifecycle explains when a thing may exist. A test explains which behavior the system is unwilling to lose. Documentation explains why those choices were made when the code can only show what they became.

The useful question is not whether an architecture looks sophisticated. It is whether someone can return years later, understand the constraints, and change the system without first reconstructing its history from accidents.

entities.title: JARVIS Alpha

entities.title: Maintainability Over Hype
entities.summary: New tools are useful when they make a system easier to understand, operate, or change—not merely newer.
body: A system accumulates history whether that history is documented or not.

Choosing maintainability over hype does not mean refusing new tools. It means asking what remains understandable after the novelty has gone: interfaces, failure modes, ownership, tests, and the reasons behind irreversible decisions.

entities.title: DNS Database
entities.summary: A production Go service for ingesting, resolving, storing, and querying domain, IP, CNAME, and reverse-DNS relationships.
entities.purpose: Maintain a durable DNS intelligence store that can absorb large file-based inputs, refresh stale records, expose predictable query interfaces, and remain operable under sustained workloads.
facts: Modular Go service with 50+ tracked test files
RocksDB-backed persistent storage
Domain and reverse-DNS file ingestion
A, AAAA, and CNAME resolution workflows
Unix-socket and REST query interfaces
Prometheus monitoring and profiling support
entities.ownership: Created and architected nearly the entire DNS Database system, including its core package structure, storage model, ingestion and resolution flows, transport interfaces, lifecycle behavior, documentation, and test strategy. Later commits and maintenance by other contributors do not represent shared creation of the underlying system.
architecture: Directory watchers turn domain and reverse-DNS files into bounded processing flows.
Resolver workers enrich records through DNS lookups with configurable retries and backoff.
RocksDB stores bidirectional domain, IP, CNAME, blacklist, and TLD relationships.
REST and Unix-socket servers expose the same query model to different consumers.
Configuration controls worker counts, channel buffers, retention, freshness, storage tuning, and shutdown behavior.
engineeringFocus: Coordinate high-volume concurrent work without leaking goroutines or losing completion signals.
Keep related indexes consistent when records are updated, merged, deleted, or refreshed.
Separate ingestion, resolution, persistence, transport, and monitoring into testable packages.
Make long-lived operations observable and safely stoppable.
Document architecture and operating constraints so the system remains maintainable beyond its original implementation.
evidence: More than 130 tracked source and support files across server, client, storage, resolver, watcher, monitoring, and configuration packages.
Unit, integration, race-oriented, and stress-oriented test suites.
Module-level architecture documentation and a detailed operator README.
Containerized build and validation flow with linting, security checks, race detection, coverage, and profiling.
entities.disclosure: Source code, internal package names, infrastructure addresses, production sizing, and organization-specific operating details are intentionally not published.

entities.title: External Playwright Adapter
entities.summary: An isolated adapter that lets independently owned Playwright suites participate in shared execution, evidence, and test-management workflows.
entities.purpose: Let teams keep specialized tests beside their own code without importing framework internals or weakening the canonical DSL-based release gate.
facts: One governed source directory maps to one managed test case
Repository, ref, and path validation before execution
Aggregates multiple Playwright specs into one managed result
Synthetic JUnit output carries source revision and authority metadata
Dry-run-first synchronization for case metadata
entities.ownership: Sole designer and implementer of the external Playwright adapter, including repository synchronization, path validation, isolated dependency installation, execution aggregation, result mapping, and CI-safe source references.
architecture: External cases are separated before canonical DSL generation begins.
The runtime validates a governed repository, revision, relative path, and required spec files.
External suites install and use their own dependencies instead of importing framework internals.
Multiple contained Playwright tests are reduced to one authoritative managed-case outcome.
Synthetic JUnit records preserve source path, revision, commit, and execution authority.
engineeringFocus: Create a removable integration boundary that does not contaminate the core generator.
Prevent test-management content from selecting arbitrary repositories or escaping configured roots.
Define deterministic semantics for failed, errored, skipped, and empty external suites.
Keep metadata synchronization reviewable through dry-run, strict validation, and explicit apply modes.
evidence: Dedicated adapter documentation and focused automated tests.
Source revision metadata travels with generated execution evidence.
CI and local execution use the same orchestration path.
entities.disclosure: Repository addresses, branch policies, job-token configuration, case metadata, and product-specific tests are private.

entities.purpose: Listed honestly as current work without inventing a public specification before one exists.

entities.title: JARVIS
entities.summary: A personal system for reducing context switching across projects, memory, documentation, automation, and creative work.
entities.purpose: Bring the different parts of long-running work into one understandable context while preserving human control at meaningful decision points.
facts: Connect projects, memory, and documentation
Coordinate automation and creative work
Reduce context switching
Keep consequential decisions behind explicit gates
entities.technologyNote: Planned architecture; expected to evolve with the project.

entities.title: Test Generation Platform
entities.summary: A company-wide QA platform that turns reviewable test contracts into deterministic Playwright execution and evidence.
entities.purpose: Make release-gate intent understandable to humans while keeping generation, execution, scheduling, and result reporting repeatable enough for CI.
facts: KiwiTCMS to DSL to IR to Playwright pipeline
Schema-driven command contracts and selector profiles
Deterministic TypeScript generation with Jinja2
Lane-aware parallel execution
JUnit, HTML, trace, and per-case video evidence
Shared Docker runtime for local, CI, and interactive use
entities.ownership: Sole creator and architect of the Playwright QA platform. Designed and implemented the generator, runtime orchestration, KiwiTCMS integration, DSL and selector contracts, CI behavior, evidence handling, scheduling, documentation, and external-case integration.
architecture: KiwiTCMS holds intended coverage and human-readable case contracts.
Normalization and schema-driven mapping translate case text into an explicit intermediate representation.
Jinja2 templates render understandable Playwright TypeScript rather than opaque runtime instructions.
Profile inheritance composes shared, product-specific, and legacy DSL, selector, and runtime layers.
A single containerized entrypoint coordinates export, lint, generation, execution, reporting, traces, and interactive debugging.
engineeringFocus: Keep the same input deterministic across normalization, mapping, rendering, and execution.
Reject ambiguous test language through lint modes, typed selector semantics, and contract tests.
Support multiple products without copying the generator or weakening the shared schema.
Schedule shared-user cases safely while preserving parallelism for independent cases.
Preserve useful evidence and map terminal test outcomes back into the test-management system.
evidence: Roughly 14,000 lines across Python, TypeScript, shell orchestration, templates, and integration tooling.
More than 20 focused generator contract-test modules plus integration-library tests.
Dedicated documentation for operators, stakeholders, DSL authors, schema contracts, selectors, troubleshooting, and adapters.
Dry-run and strict lint paths allow case authors and CI to validate contracts before execution.
entities.disclosure: The implementation, product-specific selectors, test cases, internal endpoints, and organization-specific CI configuration remain private. The architecture and engineering principles are described at a system level.

entities.name: goboot
entities.title: goboot
entities.description: Modern Go project generator for CLI, REST, and backend tools. Batteries included: clean structure, Docker, CI, and editor presets — ready in seconds.
entities.summary: Modern Go project generator for CLI, REST, and backend tools. Batteries included: clean structure, Docker, CI, and editor presets — ready in seconds.
entities.readme_excerpt: goboot A modular, versioned scaffold for reproducible Go project generation. 📦 What is goboot? goboot is a deterministic scaffolding tool for Go repositories. It focuses on explicit, layered project setup that stays maintainable as projects grow. It is not a framework or IDE. It targets teams that want reproducible output, clear service boundaries, and auditable generation behavior. 📁 Current State v0.2.0 is the…
